metadata.description: Intent-based source routing for cultural MCQ retrieval with trust levels and fallback hierarchy
metadata.author: Aditya Singh and Rickarya Das (IIT KGP, SemEval-2026 Task 7 Track 2)

trust_levels.high: Official/canonical source; appropriate as primary retrieval
trust_levels.mid: Useful supporting source; verify against high-trust sources before using as deciding evidence
trust_levels.low: For context only; should not be relied upon for MCQ correctness

primary.note: Canonical reference for leaders, parties, government structure, dates
Structured properties: head of government, official language, capital
Standardized country profiles with government sections

fallback.note: Communication style, business protocol, naming conventions (verify elsewhere)

government_politics.note: Route to official portals first if country is in country_specific_sources

primary.note: Holiday pages with dates and background; cross-check with other sources
UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage official listings (best for ceremonies/practices)

fallback.note: What happens during holidays, local customs (tourism angle)

holidays_festivals.note: Prefer UNESCO ICH for official cultural designations

primary.note: Cities, provinces, landmarks, geography facts with encyclopedic rigor
Geography sections (terrain, borders, highest point, area)
World Heritage Sites (official designation with map/location)

fallback.note: City/region sightseeing and practical descriptions (tourism perspective)

geography_places.note: Use Factbook for 'hard facts' like highest point; UNESCO for heritage sites

primary.note: Authoritative World Heritage Site descriptions and cultural significance
Intangible cultural heritage (dances, rituals, crafts, traditions)

fallback.note: Landmarks, cultural sightseeing, local context (but may be marketing-heavy)
Obscure landmarks, hidden gems, unusual sites (verify independently)
Curated museum exhibits and heritage storytelling (good for context)

culture_landmarks.note: PRIMARY: UNESCO (tangible + intangible) + Wikipedia; FALLBACK: travel guides only for supporting detail

primary.note: Dish pages, origins, standard names, ingredients (encyclopedic rigor)
When a food practice is listed as intangible heritage (e.g., tea ceremony)

fallback.note: Eat sections; local cuisine and etiquette (but tourism-oriented)
Food & drink cultural stories and places
Food in daily life, eating customs (anthropological essays)

food_drink.note: Wikipedia for dish facts; Wikivoyage for local customs only if Wikipedia is weak

sports.note: Primary sources are all high-trust; use official federation sites for sport-specific facts

primary.note: Wars, independence, major historical events, dates
Heritage framing of identity and cultural significance
Traditional practices tied to national/cultural identity

fallback.note: Historical narratives and cultural identity essays (anthropological)
Curated historical narratives and heritage context (storytelling angle)

history_identity.note: Wikipedia for facts; UNESCO for official heritage framings

primary.note: Words, slang, phrases, abbreviations, etymology (language-specific nuance)
Languages spoken in country (standardized by country)

language_writing.note: Use Wiktionary for slang/colloquial terms; Wikipedia for script/alphabet facts

primary.note: Currency, flag, anthem as RDF properties (machine-readable)
Currency pages, flag/anthem pages with narrative context
Economy sections, currency code, hard country stats
Central bank/monetary authority sites for currency codes (e.g., MAS for SGD)

economy_currency_symbols.note: Prefer central bank websites for currency; Wikidata for quick lookup

primary.note: Shows, films, songs, celebrities, plot summaries
Films, TV shows, actors, release dates (authoritative for media metadata)

fallback.note: Famous quotes and lines from films/media (verify exact wording)

media_popculture.note: Use Wikipedia + IMDb for factual film/show metadata; Wikiquote only for exact quote queries

primary.note: Detailed cross-cultural etiquette and social norms
Comprehensive coverage of denominations and history
Global museum collections and artistic history
Regional art movements and literary canons
History of fashion and traditional dress
Marriage, kinship, and gender role descriptions
Structured data on flags, emblems, and anthems
Symbolism and history of national icons
History and structure of national education
National scientific history and tech milestones
Global health standards and country profiles
Traditional medicine listed as intangible heritage

fallback.note: History of traditional medical practices (e.g., TCM, Ayurveda)

primary.note: Road, rail, and air infrastructure stats

IN.language: Hindi, English, Bengali, Telugu, Marathi, Tamil, Gujarati, Assamese, Kannada, Malayalam, Odia, Punjabi, Urdu, Sanskrit (22 official)

GB.note: NHLE is England-specific; Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland have separate heritage registries

ID.language: Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia), regional languages (Sundanese, Javanese, etc.)

sources.note: Night market culture and local cuisine
Analysis and research on North Korea

global_fallback_sources.description: Use these when country-specific and intent-specific sources are exhausted

sources.note: Anthropology-focused essays; weddings, gender roles, food, myths, daily life (verify in high-trust sources)
Cross-cultural comparison framework; etiquette, do's and don'ts, business protocol
Business/formal context; naming conventions, gift giving, communication styles
Ultimate fallback; widest coverage for any country/topic
Machine-readable structured facts for any entity

retrieval_strategy.step_1_country_intent: Extract country code and detected intent from question
retrieval_strategy.step_3_global_priority: If country not found, use global_intent_sources[intent][primary]
retrieval_strategy.step_4_fallback: If primary sources return <2 chunks, try global_intent_sources[intent][fallback]
retrieval_strategy.step_5_global_fallback: If all above exhausted, use global_fallback_sources in order of trust level
retrieval_strategy.filtering: Remove answer-key lines from text before indexing; discard any chunk containing exact MCQ option text (A/B/C/D)
